notes: Significant negative correlation (r ≈ -0.68, mediated through cognitive offloading r=+0.72 with AI use, r=-0.75 with critical thinking). Younger participants show stronger dependence + lower critical thinking
Lower cognitive load with ChatGPT but lower argument quality and reduced depth of reasoning. Acute effect — does NOT establish cumulative atrophy
Reduced neural engagement in regions associated with sustained attention and effortful cognition during LLM-assisted writing
Per-task deskilling — 17pp lower on unassisted retest. Inverts to lambda lower bound when scaled per offloading-rate × time. Most directly cumulative-relevant evidence available
Year-long field study of cancer specialists. Gradual dulling of expert judgment that does NOT show in throughput metrics. The slow-decay anchor consistent with the model's exponential ρ(t) form
Documented deskilling, cognitive offloading, and misplaced responsibility attribution in AI-assisted UX design — Bainbridge ironies-of-automation lens
Half-century of calculator use produced narrow domain offloading without long-run cognitive decline. The calculator-analogue tail of A3 (lambda ≈ 0)
